claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin your Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for.

**How old is your child?**

Please let me know if they are:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children you'd like to claim for, please tell me each of their ages and I can assess them all.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, hello! Yes, I'm hoping to find out if I can claim Child Benefit. I have one child - Alex is 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely to meet you! Thanks for sharing that information about Alex.

Since Alex is 13 years old, they fall into the **under 16** category. Let me navigate to the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good news - children under 16 generally qualify automatically for Child Benefit! But I just need to check a couple of things.

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

At 13, I'd expect this is likely the case, but I just need to confirm.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't work at all - they're just in school like any normal 13-year-old!

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's what I expected! Since Alex isn't working, let me move to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This would include benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed by Alex themselves. At 13, this would be very unusual, but I need to confirm Alex isn't receiving any such benefits.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in their own name - nothing like that at all.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Lovely! Now I need to check about apprenticeships.

**Is Alex currently doing an apprenticeship?**

At 13 and in school, I expect the answer is no, but I just need to confirm. (For your information, certain types of apprenticeships can affect eligibility, though this mainly applies to older children.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not - Alex is just 13 and in regular school. No apprenticeship or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's exactly what I thought! Let me move on to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now we need to check your residency status.

**Do you meet one of the following UK residency requirements?**

You only need to meet **one** of these to qualify:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - This is the most common situation
2. **Have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold)
4. **Have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. **Have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and family
6. **Have pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. **Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

Do any of these apply to you? Most people simply tick the first box - do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, well... that might be a problem. I don't actually live in the UK at the moment - I'm abroad right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see, thank you for letting me know. Let me ask a few follow-up questions to understand your situation better, as there are still some circumstances where you might qualify.

**Are you living abroad as a Crown servant?** (For example, working for the UK government, diplomatic service, or armed forces posted overseas?)

Or alternatively, do you have **settled or pre-settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme? If you have pre-settled status, are you:
- Working and earning above the National Insurance threshold?
- Actively seeking work?
- A student with sufficient resources?
- A family member of an EEA or Swiss national with the right to reside?

Could you tell me a bit more about your circumstances abroad? This will help me determine if any of these paths might apply to you.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that - just living abroad as a regular person. And no, I don't have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme either. I'm just living outside the UK with my family.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for being so clear about your situation. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, I need to navigate to the ineligible outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I've now gathered enough information to give you my assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to share this news, but based on our assessment:

---

## Child Benefit Eligibility Result: **Not Eligible**

### For Alex (age 13):
❌ **Not eligible** at this time

**Reason:** While Alex meets all the requirements for being a qualifying child (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not currently meet the UK residency requirements**.

To claim Child Benefit, you must either:
- Normally live in the UK, **or**
- Meet special circumstances (such as having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or being a Crown servant posted abroad)

---

### What this means for you:

Since you're currently living abroad as a regular resident without any of the qualifying statuses, you're not eligible to claim UK Child Benefit at this time.

### If your circumstances change:

If you **return to live in the UK** in the future, you would likely be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex (provided they're still under 16 or in approved education/training up to age 19). At that point, I'd encourage you to apply.

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news today. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify about the eligibility rules?

reasonings: Alex (aged 13) would normally qualify as a child under 16 who is not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled status, and does not have pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances for those living abroad.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (13), who meets all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claimant is currently living abroad and does not meet any of the UK residency requirements (not normally resident in UK, no settled/pre-settled status, not a Crown servant). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit at this time.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (aged 13) would normally qualify as a child under 16 who is not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not have settled status, and does not have pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances for those living abroad.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (13), who meets all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claimant is currently living abroad and does not meet any of the UK residency requirements (not normally resident in UK, no settled/pre-settled status, not a Crown servant). Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit at this time.
